title: Automobile Mechanic
description: An automobile mechanic in India performs routine maintenance, repairs, and overhauls of vehicles. They diagnose problems, replace faulty parts, and perform tune-ups. Mechanics often work in workshops, garages, or dealerships, and may specialize in specific types of vehicles or systems.
top_govt_colleges: National Council of Vocational Training (NCVT) Institutes
Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Kolkata
top_private_colleges: Maharashtra State Board of Technical Education (MSBTE) affiliated colleges
Andhra Pradesh State Board of Technical Education and Training (APSBTE&T) affiliated colleges

career_progression.year_0_2: Apprenticeship, basic training, and entry-level positions in workshops or garages
career_progression.year_3_5: Specialization in specific vehicle types or systems, supervisory roles, and team leadership
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior mechanic roles, technical training, and potential entrepreneurship
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting a small workshop or garage with initial investment of ₹5-10 lakhs
career_progression.global_pathway: Limited opportunities abroad, mostly in the Middle East or Africa, with a salary range of ₹15-30 lakhs per annum

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Initial struggles with finding employment, but opportunities increase with experience
common_misconceptions: Automobile mechanics are only for those who failed in academics
Mechanics can earn high salaries without experience
